South Asian Business Association

A premier business society exploring every facet of the field — built on rigorous education and an enduring community of South Asian Cornellians.

Recruitment → Our Story
Scroll

Our Mission

What we are building, and why it matters.

The South Asian Business Association at Cornell University aims to provide students with business and leadership skills through a comprehensive education process. We empower our students to explore business initiatives via mentorship from campus leaders, exclusive speaker events hosted by notable South Asian alumni, and membership in a community of lifelong friends.

About SABA

What makes us special.

Community

Join our tight-knit family to build meaningful relationships and lifelong friendships.

From wineries overlooking Cayuga Lake to nights out in the city, the bonds formed in SABA last far beyond graduation.

01 / 12
The Education Series

Experience

Complete a case-study based education series to gain real-world experience while refining interpersonal skills.

01

Weekly Cases

Tackle real business problems across finance, consulting, entrepreneurship, and beyond.

02

Weekly Presentations

Sharpen the way you think, structure, and communicate ideas under pressure.

03

Mentorship

Learn directly from accomplished South Asian Cornell students and alumni.

2022

Our Founding Story

The South Asian Business Association was founded in 2022 by Zayna Deonath ’24, Rohan Sharma ’24, and Vishwajit Sharma ’23. They saw a need at Cornell for a business organization with an innovative curriculum that ventured beyond finance and management consulting to explore all facets of the world of business — including entrepreneurship, alternative asset investing, and real estate. This mission, coupled with a desire to bring South Asian Cornellians together into an established and reliable community, led to the founding of SABA.

The three brought together many of the most accomplished South Asian Cornell students and alumni as mentors to bolster a carefully designed education process, and proudly inaugurated its first New Member Class in the Fall of 2022.

Zayna Deonath ’24 Rohan Sharma ’24 Vishwajit Sharma ’23
